channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Activision débranche Warzone Mobile : le jeu a fait un flop sur smartphone
item.description: Clap de fin pour le battle royale d'Activision. Ce 17 avril 2026, les serveurs de Call of Duty Warzone Mobile ont été officiellement et définitivement débranchés. À peine deux ans après son lancement mondial, le titre fait ses adieux, victime d'un public mobile qui n'a jamais vraiment répondu présent.

item.title: « L’IA va tuer l’open source » : l’alerte du populaire projet Cal
item.description: L'émergence de l'IA générative, en particulier dans la programmation d'applications, continue de perturber le marché. D'après Cal, un projet populaire open source, elle représente la mort de l'open source.

item.title: On a essayé de m’arnaquer avec une photo IA et un vocal envoyés par SMS : attention à cette nouvelle mode très piégeuse
item.description: Une photo d'un colis générée avec l'intelligence artificielle, et un vocal d'une dizaine de secondes probablement, lui aussi, fait avec l'IA, le tout envoyé par SMS : voici la nouvelle technique utilisée par les scammeurs pour dérober vos données personnelles. La vigilance est de mise.

item.title: ExoMars : après vingt ans de galères, l’Europe a enfin son rover pour Mars
item.description: Le rover Rosalind Franklin est prêt depuis 2022. Il attend dans un hangar italien que l'Europe lui trouve un chauffeur pour Mars.

item.title: Fnac casse le prix de la Xiaomi Electric Scooter 5 Pro, une trottinette endurante sur 60 km
item.description: Avec son autonomie allant jusqu’à 60 km, la Xiaomi Electric Scooter 5 Pro s’impose comme une trottinette électrique particulièrement adaptée aux trajets du quotidien comme aux déplacements plus longs. Même si elle a depuis été remplacée dans la gamme, elle reste une valeur sûre surtout maintenant qu'elle revient à 399 euros seulement chez la Fnac. ...

item.title: The ‘AI is inevitable’ trap
item.description: In the latest sign of AI silly season, Allbirds, the shoe company, told the world it was now an AI company and briefly managed to septuple its stock price. The Newbird AI story is really just one of a bunch of things this week that made us wonder: have we reached the peak of AI, […]

item.title: Bluesky confirms DDoS attack is cause of continued app outages
item.description: Bluesky has been experiencing ongoing service disruptions since just before 3 a.m. ET. on April 15.

item.title: TCL Says Screw All Those New RGB TVs, Even Its Own
item.description: TCL now has its own RGB TV that it explicitly says offers less value than its new 'super quantum dot' mini LED screens.

item.title: Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream devs say there was 'a big debate' about whether Miis should fart — 'We really obsessed over getting the sound just right'
item.description: The Tomodachi Life: Living the Dreamcreators have revealed that there was a debate between the development team about whether they should allow Miis to fart.

item.title: MAGA Is Increasingly Convinced the Trump Assassination Attempt Was Staged
item.description: Conspiracy theories about the Butler, Pennsylvania, shooting have ramped up in recent weeks as once steadfast Trump supporters turn on the president.

item.title: Netflix plans to add a vertical video feed, use AI for recommendations
item.description: Netflix is going to launch a TikTok-like vertical video feed within its apps this month, and plans to use AI broadly for content creation and recommendations.

item.title: A Starlink outage left dozens of unmanned US Navy vessels stranded off the California coast — and exposed the Pentagon's worrying reliance on Elon Musk
item.description: 'You accept those vulnerabilities because of the benefits you get from the ubiquity Starlink provides' — and it's the only realistic option right now.

item.title: Samsung finally explains why it stopped making small phones — and has a suggestion for fans of compact handsets
item.description: Samsung stopped making small phones because people needed larger screens, but there's still a relatively compact option to pick from.

item.title: Microsoft experts warn North Korean attackers target macOS users with 'a highly reliable infection chain' to steal passwords, financial data and more — here's how to stay safe
item.description: A Lazarus spinoff is stirring trouble among companies, stealing crypto through fake jobs, Microsoft warns.

item.title: It's marathon season, and Amazon is slashing prices on best-selling running shoes and watches — up to 55% off Hoka, Nike, Garmin, and Apple
item.description: Prep for marathon season with Amazon's weekend sale on best-selling running shoes and watches, with up to 55% from Hoka, Nike, Garmin, Apple, and more.

item.title: The ‘TMNT: Mutant Mayhem’ Sequel Has Found Its Villains
item.description: Plus, Takashi Yamazak talks Godzilla's attack on New York in 'Minus Zero'.

item.title: SaySo is a new short-form video app that aims to restore users’ trust in news
item.description: Users are fed up with misinformation and AI slop cluttering their feeds. SaySo is a new short-form video app that delivers news from vetted creators and journalists.

item.title: Loop raises $95M to build supply chain AI that predicts disruptions
item.description: The San Francisco startup closed a Series C funding round led by Antonio Gracias' firm Valor, which is a major backer of xAI.

item.title: TCL's RGB-Mini LED TVs will start at $8,000
item.description: TCL introduced its new flagship X11L SQD-Mini LED TV at CES 2026, and now a few months later, the company is ready to expand its lineup with more SQD-Mini LED models and its first RGB-Mini LED TV. All sizes of the TCL QM8L SQD-Mini LED TV are available now. Meanwhile, both the TCL QM7L SQD-Mini LED TVs and the RM9L RGB-Mini LED TVs are available to pre-order. SQD-Mini LED panels are TCL's latest iteration of its Mini LED display technology, where "SQD" stands for "Super Quantum Dot," a layer of tiny crystal dots that help filter the light from the LEDs in the company's panels. TCL claims its S...

item.title: The creative software industry has declared war on Adobe
item.description: All empires eventually fall, and it seems the creative software industry has collectively decided that Adobe's time has come. The Creative Cloud provider's suite of design tools have been considered the industry standard for decades - despite unpopular decisions to fully embrace generative AI and abandon software licenses in favor of expensive, complicated subscriptions. Pricing […]...

item.title: ‘This was inevitable’: Meta blames RAM crisis for Quest 3 and Quest 3S price hikes, but fans still think they’re still a good deal
item.description: The Meta Quest 3 and Quest 3S are getting more expensive as Meta blames the RAM crisis.

item.title: Why doesn't Canva just use GPT, Claude, Gemini...?
item.description: Training and using its own models is actually good economics for Canva, and it means you can get its design tools for less.

item.title: 'Fresh' or 'hideous'? Insta360's first mirrorless camera has leaked again — and photographers can't decide if they love it or hate it
item.description: The latest leaked device from Insta360 is a mirrorless camera that may be part of a wider trend in the market.

item.title: Coolfly Aura Review: More Angles, Fewer Advantages
item.description: Coolfly’s Aura is colorful, clever, and flexible, but its signature feature ends up being more hassle than help.

item.title: I've picked out 6 superb turntables at all price points for Record Store Day — here’s how to level up your vinyls
item.description: What better time is there to upgrade your turntable than on Record Store Day? None, there's no better time.

item.title: 'The serious audiophile Record Store Day deck': this elite new turntable unnerves me almost as much as it piques my interest
item.description: TechDas' elite new turntable vacuum-clamps your vinyl to a heavyweight platter floating on air, promising to dig up 'extraordinary sound' from an LP’s grooves

item.title: Netflix s’attaque à TikTok et YouTube : la plateforme de SVoD annonce l’arrivée imminente des vidéos verticales
item.description: Annoncées l'année dernière, les vidéos verticales arrivent très bientôt sur l'application mobile de Netflix. Lors de la présentation de ses résultats trimestriels, le géant du streaming a annoncé un déploiement dès la fin du mois d'avril.

item.title: Netflix stock faces a punishing day as Reed Hastings departs. Don’t blame his exit on WBD, bosses say
item.description: In its first earnings report since it walked away from the mega deal, the streaming giant shared unexpected news that investors clearly don’t love. Shares of Netflix Inc. (Nasdaq: NFLX) are getting battered this morning, one day after the company reported its Q1 2026 financial results—the first since the streaming giant abandoned its plans to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) in February....

item.title: How Bob Odenkirk and the ‘Nobody’ team set out to elevate the genre with ‘Normal’
item.description: Bob Odenkirk, Ben Wheatley, Derek Kolstad, and Marc Provissiero sit down with Mashable's Kristy Puchko to discuss their gritty, old-school approach to filmmaking in 'Normal'.

item.title: The 4TB SanDisk Extreme is one of the best rugged portable SSDs we've ever tested — and it’s over $300 off right now
item.description: Loads of room for files and a rugged design that can take life's hard knocks.

item.title: Le Google Pixel 10a vient de sortir et s’affiche déjà à prix cassé avec un casque JBL endurant offert
item.description: Google a récemment dévoilé son nouveau fleuron de milieu de gamme, le Pixel 10a, qui est quasiment une copie du Pixel 9a. Actuellement, Boulanger le propose dans un pack avec un casque audio JBL Tune 520BT à 449 euros au lieu de 608,99 euros.

item.title: 'Codex can now operate your computer alongside you' OpenAI takes major shot at Claude Code with major workplace updates
item.description: OpenAI wants Codex to be an even smarter and more effective AI co-worker.

item.title: ‘The Dog Stars’ Brings Ridley Scott to a Mysterious Apocalypse
item.description: The gritty sci-fi survival tale starring Jacob Elordi, Margaret Qualley, and Josh Brolin has just dropped its first trailer.

item.title: Best Bluetooth Speakers (2026): JBL, Sonos, Bose, and More
item.description: These are our favorite portable speakers of all shapes and sizes, from clip-ons to a massive boom box.

item.title: Should you attend a conference if you’re not speaking?
item.description: Thirteen leaders share how they make the decision. Time is precious, and conferences can be expensive—and time-consuming. If your name is not on the official agenda, should you attend anyway? Perhaps it’s an annual industry gathering, or it’s a niche conference that may bring in business. There are many reasons to attend, and just as many not to.

item.title: Getting started with Starlink Roam
item.description: Learn how to set up Starlink Roam, test speeds, and use it on the go. Tips for satellite internet while traveling.

item.title: Engadget Podcast: Allbirds goes from shoes to AI
item.description: So a shoe company turned into an AI company…. That’s it, that’s the joke. In this episode, Devindra chats with Engadget’s Daniel Cooper about Allbirds’ sudden transformation and what it says about the AI economy. Also, they chat about the Artemis II moon mission, Meta being warned about the dangers of facial recognition (again) and how teens think social media is really shaping them.Subscribe!iTunesSpotifyPocket CastsStitcherGoogle PodcastsTopicNear-dead shoe company Allbirds is doing AI now – 1:47Artemis II safely returns to Earth, did you know they had DSLRs and iPhones up there? – 15:57Meta...

item.title: The first-gen AirPods Max drop to a record-low price following AirPods Max 2 launch
item.description: The original AirPods Max have dropped to a new record-low price following the launch of the second-generation model of Apple's headphones.

item.title: NASAs Artemis II mission concludes with a perfect splashdown in the Pacific
item.description: NASA’s Artemis II mission ends with a Pacific Ocean splashdown. The Orion capsule safely returns astronauts after a historic lunar journey.

item.title: 'Genuinely might be the worst trailer I've ever seen': new Netflix movie Ladies First slammed by outraged fans as woman dominated world becomes man's 'worst nightmare'
item.description: New Netflix movie Ladies First explores and man's worst nightmare in a woman dominated world — but its first trailer hasn't gone down well.

item.title: Down from $1,299, the 32-inch 4K OLED ASUS ROG Swift gaming monitor is under $900 at Amazon
item.description: Get the ASUS ROG Swift 32-inch 4K OLED monitor for $899.99 at Amazon, down from $1,299 with dual-mode 240Hz/480Hz play.
